title: moraea regalis
intro: Moraea regalis, also known as the "royal moraea," is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. It is a bulbous perennial that is native to the western and southern parts of South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating a variety of health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea or infusion, or dried and used as a powder to be taken orally or applied topically, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
